posts.postAngle: The algorithm rewards watch time, not buyer intent.
posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer will optimize for buyer search instead of the watch-time algorithm.
posts.hook: Stop trying to beat the YouTube algorithm. For B2B, you are grinding at a game you do not even want to win.

posts.postAngle: A ranked video keeps converting for years.
posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer will treat a video as a compounding asset, not a disposable post.
posts.hook: A LinkedIn post dies in 48 hours. A YouTube video kept booking us demos 18 months later.

posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer will make evergreen search videos instead of chasing trends.
posts.hook: Chasing trending topics is the fastest way to build a channel nobody searches for next month.

posts.postAngle: The search bar names the videos your buyers want.
posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer can pull exact buyer phrases from YouTube autocomplete instead of guessing.
posts.hook: Your buyers will tell you exactly what to make. For free. You just have to read YouTube's search bar instead of guessing

posts.postAngle: Buyers run problem/solution/vendor searches; channels answer only the first.
posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer can sort videos by the three buyer-search stages and fill the pipeline gap.
posts.hook: Your buyer runs 3 searches before they buy. Your channel probably answers 1.

posts.sourceTitle: Ahrefs — YouTube SEO for AI Search
posts.postAngle: YouTube is the most-cited domain in Google's AI Overviews (Ahrefs).
posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer will make videos that answer the questions AI tools cite, to get found inside AI answers.
posts.hook: Your buyers have started asking AI instead of searching Google. And the AI keeps quoting one place most B2B teams ignore

posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer will make an honest comparison video, because those viewers are at the deciding moment.
posts.hook: One "us vs them" video booked more demos than a year of our thought-leadership. It had the fewest views on the channel.

posts.postAngle: Videos with no next step waste the trust they earn.
posts.thesis: After this post, a founder can give every video one specific next step so viewers become leads.
posts.hook: Your best video probably has no idea what to do next. So neither does the viewer.

posts.postAngle: Titles written for humans miss the buyer’s search.
posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer can lead a title with the buyer’s literal search phrase so the right people find it.
posts.hook: Your best video is invisible. The title did it.

posts.postAngle: Every video does one of two jobs: get found or get chosen.
posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer can tag their last 10 videos as get-found or get-chosen and fix the missing half.
posts.hook: Your YouTube channel does 1 of 2 jobs. Most B2B channels only do the first.

posts.postAngle: Your competitor already found the topics that bring them customers.
posts.thesis: After this post, a marketer can reverse-engineer a competitor's best videos to find proven buyer-intent topics.
posts.hook: Your competitor already found the video topics that bring them customers.

posts.postAngle: YouTube out-converted blogging 3.25x for the same business.
posts.thesis: After this post, the reader will judge channels by in-market viewers, not traffic.
posts.hook: "YouTube's great for brand awareness. Blogging is what actually drives leads."

posts.sourceTitle: YouTube Marketing ROI: Formula, Real Case Study Data + Free Calculator
posts.postAngle: YouTube ROI is calculable but most B2B teams guess instead of measuring. Real case study data shows 3.25x conversion advantage over blogging.

posts.sourceTitle: Why Most YouTube Strategies Fail (And What Actually Works)
posts.postAngle: Creator strategy advice actively harms businesses on YouTube — the 7 failure patterns and what the acquisition-first model looks like instead
posts.hook: Your YouTube channel might be working perfectly for the wrong goal.

posts.sourceTitle: How We Engineer High-Intent YouTube Topics: Inside Our Opportunity Analysis Framework
posts.postAngle: The metric businesses use to pick YouTube topics (search volume) is the wrong one — intent density is what drives leads
posts.hook: The number that's ruining most YouTube content strategies isn't views, subscribers, or watch time.
